_provenance.stage: Stage 1 — derived from live cards for retroactive Stage 2/3 verification
_provenance.note: This Stage 1 JSON was reconstructed from the currently-published cards so external Stage 2 (Gemini bias) and Stage 3 (Codex fact-check) reviews can verify what readers see. It is not the original analysis — the original pipeline was not run on this issue.

headline: Every Election. No Rules. No Receipts.
context: Malaysia has held 15 general elections without a campaign finance law. Parties are not required to disclose donors, spending, or funding sources.

cards.text: Malaysia has no campaign finance law. None.
cards.sub: No disclosure. No spending limits. No donor restrictions. Since 1957.

cards.h: GE15 estimated spending: RM1.2–2.5 billion across all parties.
cards.s: The range is a guess. No party is required to report anything.
cards.d: GE15 estimated spending: RM1.2–2.5 billion across all parties. The range is a guess. No party is required to report anything.

cards.h: MACC recommended campaign finance legislation three times since 2019.
cards.s: Each time, the sitting government declined. Every party benefits from opacity.
cards.d: MACC recommended campaign finance legislation three times since 2019. Each time, the sitting government declined. Every party benefits from opacity.

cards.h: Indonesia caps spending and requires disclosure. Thailand publishes donor lists.
cards.s: Malaysia is the only major ASEAN democracy with zero campaign finance regulation.
cards.d: Indonesia caps spending and requires disclosure. Thailand publishes donor lists. Malaysia is the only major ASEAN democracy with zero campaign finance regulation.

cards.text: If you do not know who funded the winner, do you know who governs?
Like a football league where teams never publish their spending. You watch the match but never know who bought the result.
cards.sub: Without campaign finance laws, voters cannot distinguish policy from patronage

cards.text: No party will legislate against its own funding advantage. This requires public pressure, not political goodwill.

confidence: Low — derived from published cards, original brief not on disk; submit for retroactive Stage 2/3 verification
